zh.Horton, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾霍頓
zh.lifeSummary: Henry de Horton是Roger Horton的兒子，於1215年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾霍頓。他於1239年與Lady Elia de Horton在英國爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1270年1月13日在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾沃爾頓享年55歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Horton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Warwickshire): habitational name from one or other of the many places so called, such as those in Berkshire, Buckinghamshire, Dorset, Gloucestershire, Herefordshire, Northamptonshire, Shropshire, Somerset, Staffordshire, Wiltshire, and Yorkshire. Most of the placenames derive from Old English horh or horu ‘dirt, filth’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some may have different origins, including Horton in Gloucestershire, which may derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + tūn.

variants.name: Horten
meanings: Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farms named Horten, from an unattested word denoting ‘uneven, hard and lumpy soil’.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

en.lifeSummary: Henry de Horton was born in 1215, in Horton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Roger Horton. He married Lady Elia de Horton in 1239, in Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 13 January 1270, in Walton, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55.

ko.lifeSummary: Henry de Horton(은)는 1215년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 호르턴에서 Roger Horton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1239년 영국 체실르에서 Lady Elia de Horton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1270년 1월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 월턴에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.
